title: Develop software program code according to ready specifications of software requirements
glossary: API (Application Programming Interface)
A defined set of methods, protocols and data formats through which software components or external systems interact with one another.
Business logic
The part of a program that encodes the real-world rules and computations of the application domain, separate from the user interface and data storage.
Design pattern
A reusable, proven solution to a commonly occurring problem in software design, such as Singleton, Factory, Observer or Strategy.
Exception handling
A mechanism for detecting, signaling and responding to runtime errors in a controlled way using constructs such as try, catch/except and finally.
Framework
A reusable software platform that provides a skeleton of ready components and control flow on which an application is built.
Localization (l10n)
The adaptation of software to a particular language, region and cultural conventions, including translation, formats of date, number and currency.
ORM (Object-Relational Mapping)
A technique that maps objects in code to rows in relational database tables, allowing data to be manipulated through objects rather than SQL.
Refactoring
The disciplined process of restructuring existing code to improve its readability and internal design without changing its external behavior.
Requirement specification
A structured document that describes the functional and non-functional requirements a software product must satisfy.
Test case
A set of input data, execution conditions and expected results used to verify that a piece of software behaves according to its requirements.
User interface (UI)
The part of a system through which users interact with the program, including screens, controls, layout and interaction flow.
Version control
A system such as Git that records changes to source code over time and supports collaboration, branching and merging.
